Describe 'New-UndoScript' {
    BeforeEach {
        $script:testDir = Join-Path ([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) "rename-test-$([guid]::NewGuid())"
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $script:testDir | Out-Null
        New-Item -ItemType File -Path (Join-Path $script:testDir 'report_2024.txt') | Out-Null
        New-Item -ItemType File -Path (Join-Path $script:testDir 'report_2025.txt') | Out-Null
    }

    AfterEach {
        Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $script:testDir -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }

    It 'Should generate a valid PowerShell undo script file' {
        [PSCustomObject[]]$plan = @(Get-RenamePreview -Path $script:testDir -Pattern 'report_(\d+)' -Replacement 'rpt_$1')
        [string]$undoPath = Join-Path $script:testDir 'undo.ps1'

        New-UndoScript -RenamePlan $plan -OutputPath $undoPath

        (Test-Path $undoPath) | Should -BeTrue
        [string]$content = Get-Content -LiteralPath $undoPath -Raw
        # The undo script should rename back: rpt_2024.txt -> report_2024.txt
        $content | Should -Match 'rpt_2024\.txt'
        $content | Should -Match 'report_2024\.txt'
    }

    It 'Should produce an undo script that actually reverses renames' {
        [PSCustomObject[]]$plan = @(Get-RenamePreview -Path $script:testDir -Pattern 'report_(\d+)' -Replacement 'rpt_$1')
        [string]$undoPath = Join-Path $script:testDir 'undo.ps1'

        # Generate undo script, then perform the rename
        New-UndoScript -RenamePlan $plan -OutputPath $undoPath
        Invoke-FileRename -RenamePlan $plan

        # Verify files were renamed
        (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:testDir 'rpt_2024.txt')) | Should -BeTrue
        (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:testDir 'report_2024.txt')) | Should -BeFalse

        # Run the undo script
        & $undoPath

        # Originals should be restored
        (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:testDir 'report_2024.txt')) | Should -BeTrue
        (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:testDir 'report_2025.txt')) | Should -BeTrue
        # Renamed versions should be gone
        (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:testDir 'rpt_2024.txt')) | Should -BeFalse
        (Test-Path (Join-Path $script:testDir 'rpt_2025.txt')) | Should -BeFalse
    }

    It 'Should include strict mode in the generated undo script' {
        [PSCustomObject[]]$plan = @(Get-RenamePreview -Path $script:testDir -Pattern 'report_(\d+)' -Replacement 'rpt_$1')
        [string]$undoPath = Join-Path $script:testDir 'undo.ps1'

        New-UndoScript -RenamePlan $plan -OutputPath $undoPath

        [string]$content = Get-Content -LiteralPath $undoPath -Raw
        $content | Should -Match 'Set-StrictMode'
        $content | Should -Match 'ErrorActionPreference'
    }
}

function New-UndoScript {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Generates a PowerShell script that reverses a set of file renames.
    .DESCRIPTION
        Takes a rename plan and writes a .ps1 file that, when executed,
        renames each file back to its original name.
    #>
    [CmdletBinding()]
    [OutputType([void])]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [PSCustomObject[]]$RenamePlan,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$OutputPath
    )

    [System.Text.StringBuilder]$sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
    [void]$sb.AppendLine('# Auto-generated undo script — reverses file renames')
    [void]$sb.AppendLine('Set-StrictMode -Version Latest')
    [void]$sb.AppendLine('$ErrorActionPreference = ''Stop''')
    [void]$sb.AppendLine('')

    foreach ($entry in $RenamePlan) {
        # Undo: rename from NewName back to OldName
        [string]$escapedNewPath = ([string]$entry.FullNewPath).Replace("'", "''")
        [string]$escapedOldName = ([string]$entry.OldName).Replace("'", "''")
        [void]$sb.AppendLine("Rename-Item -LiteralPath '$escapedNewPath' -NewName '$escapedOldName'")
    }

    [void]$sb.AppendLine('')
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("Write-Host 'Undo complete: $($RenamePlan.Count) file(s) restored.'")

    Set-Content -LiteralPath $OutputPath -Value ([string]$sb.ToString()) -Encoding UTF8
}

1	Set-StrictMode -Version Latest
2	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
3	
4	# Batch File Renamer — regex-based file renaming with preview, undo, and conflict detection.
5	
6	function Get-RenamePreview {
7	    <#
8	    .SYNOPSIS
9	        Previews regex-based file renames without modifying files on disk.
10	    .DESCRIPTION
11	        Scans files in the given directory, applies the regex pattern to each filename,
12	        and returns objects describing what would be renamed.
13	    #>
14	    [CmdletBinding()]
15	    [OutputType([PSCustomObject[]])]
16	    param(
17	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
18	        [string]$Path,
19	
20	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
21	        [string]$Pattern,
22	
23	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
24	        [string]$Replacement
25	    )
26	
27	    if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $Path -PathType Container)) {
28	        throw "Directory not found: $Path"
29	    }
30	
31	    [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]$results = [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]::new()
32	
33	    [System.IO.FileInfo[]]$files = Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $Path -File
34	
35	    foreach ($file in $files) {
36	        [string]$oldName = $file.Name
37	        if ($oldName -match $Pattern) {
38	            [string]$newName = [regex]::Replace($oldName, $Pattern, $Replacement)
39	            if ($newName -ne $oldName) {
40	                $results.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
41	                    OldName = [string]$oldName
42	                    NewName = [string]$newName
43	                    FullOldPath = [string]$file.FullName
44	                    FullNewPath = [string](Join-Path $Path $newName)
45	                })
46	            }
47	        }
48	    }
49	
50	    return [PSCustomObject[]]$results.ToArray()
51	}
52	
53	function Find-RenameConflicts {
54	    <#
55	    .SYNOPSIS
56	        Detects naming conflicts in a rename plan.
57	    .DESCRIPTION
58	        Checks for two types of conflicts:
59	        1. Two source files would both rename to the same target name.
60	        2. A target name already exists on disk and that file is not being renamed away.
61	    #>
62	    [CmdletBinding()]
63	    [OutputType([PSCustomObject[]])]
64	    param(
65	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
66	        [PSCustomObject[]]$RenamePlan
67	    )
68	
69	    [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]$conflicts = [System.Collections.Generic.List[PSCustomObject]]::new()
70	
71	    # Build a set of old names (files that will be renamed away from their current name)
72	    [System.Collections.Generic.HashSet[string]]$oldNames = [System.Collections.Generic.HashSet[string]]::new(
73	        [System.StringComparer]::OrdinalIgnoreCase
74	    )
75	    foreach ($entry in $RenamePlan) {
76	        [void]$oldNames.Add([string]$entry.OldName)
77	    }
78	
79	    # Group by new name to find duplicates
80	    [hashtable]$targetCounts = @{}
81	    foreach ($entry in $RenamePlan) {
82	        [string]$key = [string]$entry.NewName
83	        if ($targetCounts.ContainsKey($key)) {
84	            [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]$list = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]$targetCounts[$key]
85	            $list.Add([string]$entry.OldName)
86	        } else {
87	            [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]$list = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
88	            $list.Add([string]$entry.OldName)
89	            $targetCounts[$key] = $list
90	        }
91	    }
92	
93	    foreach ($kvp in $targetCounts.GetEnumerator()) {
94	        [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]$sources = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]$kvp.Value
95	        if ($sources.Count -gt 1) {
96	            $conflicts.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
97	                ConflictingNewName = [string]$kvp.Key
98	                Sources            = [string[]]$sources.ToArray()
99	                Reason             = [string]"Multiple files would rename to the same name"
100	            })
101	        }
102	    }
103	
104	    # Check if any target name collides with an existing file not being renamed
105	    foreach ($entry in $RenamePlan) {
106	        [string]$targetPath = [string]$entry.FullNewPath
107	        if ((Test-Path -LiteralPath $targetPath) -and (-not $oldNames.Contains([string]$entry.NewName))) {
108	            $conflicts.Add([PSCustomObject]@{
109	                ConflictingNewName = [string]$entry.NewName
110	                Sources            = [string[]]@([string]$entry.OldName)
111	                Reason             = [string]"Target file already exists and is not being renamed"
112	            })
113	        }
114	    }
115	
116	    return [PSCustomObject[]]$conflicts.ToArray()
117	}
118	
119	function Invoke-FileRename {
120	    <#
121	    .SYNOPSIS
122	        Executes a rename plan, renaming files on disk.
123	    .DESCRIPTION
124	        Takes a plan from Get-RenamePreview and performs the actual renames.
125	        Checks for conflicts first and aborts if any are found.
126	    #>
127	    [CmdletBinding()]
128	    [OutputType([void])]
129	    param(
130	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
131	        [PSCustomObject[]]$RenamePlan
132	    )
133	
134	    # Validate all source files exist before starting
135	    foreach ($entry in $RenamePlan) {
136	        if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath ([string]$entry.FullOldPath))) {
137	            throw "Source file not found: $([string]$entry.FullOldPath)"
138	        }
139	    }
140	
141	    # Check for conflicts before performing any renames
142	    [PSCustomObject[]]$conflicts = @(Find-RenameConflicts -RenamePlan $RenamePlan)
143	    if ($conflicts.Count -gt 0) {
144	        [string]$msg = "Rename aborted due to conflict: $([string]$conflicts[0].ConflictingNewName) — $([string]$conflicts[0].Reason)"
145	        throw $msg
146	    }
147	
148	    # Perform the renames
149	    foreach ($entry in $RenamePlan) {
150	        Rename-Item -LiteralPath ([string]$entry.FullOldPath) -NewName ([string]$entry.NewName)
151	    }
152	}
153	
154	function New-UndoScript {
155	    <#
156	    .SYNOPSIS
157	        Generates a PowerShell script that reverses a set of file renames.
158	    .DESCRIPTION
159	        Takes a rename plan and writes a .ps1 file that, when executed,
160	        renames each file back to its original name.
161	    #>
162	    [CmdletBinding()]
163	    [OutputType([void])]
164	    param(
165	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
166	        [PSCustomObject[]]$RenamePlan,
167	
168	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
169	        [string]$OutputPath
170	    )
171	
172	    [System.Text.StringBuilder]$sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
173	    [void]$sb.AppendLine('# Auto-generated undo script — reverses file renames')
174	    [void]$sb.AppendLine('Set-StrictMode -Version Latest')
175	    [void]$sb.AppendLine('$ErrorActionPreference = ''Stop''')
176	    [void]$sb.AppendLine('')
177	
178	    foreach ($entry in $RenamePlan) {
179	        # Undo: rename from NewName back to OldName
180	        [string]$escapedNewPath = ([string]$entry.FullNewPath).Replace("'", "''")
181	        [string]$escapedOldName = ([string]$entry.OldName).Replace("'", "''")
182	        [void]$sb.AppendLine("Rename-Item -LiteralPath '$escapedNewPath' -NewName '$escapedOldName'")
183	    }
184	
185	    [void]$sb.AppendLine('')
186	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("Write-Host 'Undo complete: $($RenamePlan.Count) file(s) restored.'")
187	
188	    Set-Content -LiteralPath $OutputPath -Value ([string]$sb.ToString()) -Encoding UTF8
189	}

All **20 tests pass**. Here's a summary of what was built using TDD:

**`Rename-Files.ps1`** — 4 functions, all with `[CmdletBinding()]`, `[OutputType()]`, explicit types, and strict mode:

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Get-RenamePreview` | Scans directory, applies regex to filenames, returns `OldName`/`NewName` objects without touching disk |
| `Find-RenameConflicts` | Detects two conflict types: duplicate targets (two files → same name) and collisions with existing files not being renamed |
| `Invoke-FileRename` | Executes the rename plan after validating sources exist and no conflicts remain; aborts entirely on conflict (no partial renames) |
| `New-UndoScript` | Generates a standalone `.ps1` script (with strict mode) that reverses the renames |

**`Rename-Files.Tests.ps1`** — 20 Pester tests across 5 `Describe` blocks covering:
- Preview correctness (matching, non-matching, empty results)
- Conflict detection (duplicate targets, existing-file collisions, clean plans)
- Actual rename execution (success, conflict abort, missing source)
- Undo script generation and execution (round-trip verification)
- Edge cases (spaces in names, multiple capture groups, no-op patterns, empty dirs, invalid paths)
